data.title: Resilience in Europe, outlook for cocoa futures earns upgrade for Mondelez
data.description:
Mondelez (NASDAQ:MDLZ) is showing resilience in Europe, with minimal demand erosion despite price increases. Supported by solid execution and a more favorable cocoa price outlook, Jefferies upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold and raised its price target to $78.

“Europe remains a key growth engine [for Mondelez] and we forecast [double-digit %] organic growth in Q2/rest of 2025, with upside possible should limited volume declines enable more cost recovery than expected,” Jefferies analyst Scott Marks wrote in his research note to clients.

In the U.S., however, demand was still subdued due to ongoing consumer strain with channel data for the twelve weeks ending June 14 showing sales down low-single-digits, slightly worse than the prior period. Volume share has followed suit, down 32 basis points year over year versus a 5 basis point improvement during the first quarter, as share loss in cookies has accelerated.

But while North American volumes could remain soft for the rest of the year, Marks believes Mondelez (NASDAQ:MDLZ) has been doing more than peers in terms of revenue growth management, which includes innovation, channel mix, pricing/promotion, and price-pack-architecture.

Looking at a key ingredient for Mondelez, cocoa futures' outlook will improve in 2026 which suggests Mondelez’s cost environment is also improving. Coupled with advantaged end market category positioning, pricing power, brand support, and revenue growth management capabilities, Jefferies believes the company has upside earnings potential over the next three years versus peers.

The outlook leads the analyst to raise his EPS estimates for Mondelez (NASDAQ:MDLZ) for Q3 to $0.81, to $0.79 for Q4 and to $3.03 for FY25, from $0.79, $0.78, and $3.00, respectively.

Mondelez (MDLZ) reports Q2 results after the market close on July 29, expected to show an adjusted profit of $0.68 per share on $8.81B in sales. 

Shares are up 2.3%.

data.title: Market Voices: Lucid-Uber robotaxis, Warsh on Fed "regime change"
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's daily roundup of statements, announcements and remarks that could impact markets or individual stocks. 

  • Uber (NYSE:UBER) said it plans to invest millions in EV maker Lucid (NASDAQ:LCID) as part of a new partnership to expand its robotaxi offerings.

Under the deal, Lucid will supply Uber with more than 20,000 EVs over a six-year period that utilize autonomous driving technology developed by Google-backed startup Nuro. Uber and Lucid plan to launch the robotaxi service next year.  

According to CNBC, the investment will be worth around $300 million. 

“We’re thrilled to partner with Nuro and Lucid on this new robotaxi program, purpose-built just for the Uber platform, to safely bring the magic of autonomous driving to more people across the world,” said Uber (NYSE:UBER) CEO Dara Khosrowshahi in a statement.

"This investment from Uber further validates Lucid's fully redundant zonal architecture and highly capable platform as ideal for autonomous vehicles, and our industry-leading range and spacious, well-appointed interiors, as ideal for ridesharing," said Lucid (NASDAQ:LCID) interim CEO Marc Winterhoff in the same statement. "This is the start of our path to extend our innovation and technology leadership into this multi-trillion-dollar market."

Uber (UBER) also has a robotaxi partnership with Google's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) Waymo. 

  • Former Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh, who is believed to be a leading candidate to replace Jerome Powell as Fed chair, is calling for "regime change" at the central bank.

“We need regime change in the conduct of policy,” Warsh said during an interview with CNBC on Thursday. “The credibility deficit lies with the incumbents that are at the Fed, in my view.”

Warsh also indicated that he would be at odds with several of the Federal Reserve governors if he were to become chair.

“Their hesitancy to cut rates, I think, is actually quite a quite a mark against them,” Warsh said during the interview. “The specter of the miss they made on inflation, it has stuck with them. So one of the reasons why the President, I think, is right to be pushing the Fed publicly is we need regime change in the conduct of policy.”

data.title: Brixmor Property upgraded to Outperform at Mizuho on strong growth outlook
data.description:
Mizuho raised its rating on Brixmor Property Group (NYSE:BRX) to Outperform from Neutral due to the shopping REIT's "defensive tenancy" and robust growth profile owing to its low rent basis/high spreads and earnings visibility from its "signed, not occupied" pipeline.

The upgrade comes as analyst Haendel St. Juste turns more constructive on the shopping center REIT sector, due to a more positive set-up in 2025's second half. "For the sector, we believe Shopping Center portfolios are 'well-enough' positioned from a tenant credit-reserve perspective to raise their initial FY25 guidance ranges and also expect improved occupancy/SNO (signed not occupied) tailwinds to drive improved growth into 2H25," he wrote in a note to clients.

Furthermore, Mizuho believes Brixmor (NYSE:BRX) "has ample 'cushion' related to tenant credit risk, suggesting upside to 2025 estimates/guidance, and see(s) near-term bankruptcies as an opportunity to re-lease at wide spreads," he added.

And even though the stock has outperformed its peers by ~300 basis points year-to-date, it still has one of the lowest multiples in the subsector, he added.

 

St. Juste's Outperform rating contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Hold and aligns with the average SA Analyst rating and average Wall Street rating, both at Buy.

Brixmor (BRX) stock rose 0.2% in Thursday morning trading.

data.title: Zuckerberg, Meta investors reach settlement to end $8B Cambridge Analytica trial
data.description:
Mark Zuckerberg and certain senior executives of Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) have agreed to settle a lawsuit related to violations by Cambridge Analytica, according to a Reuters report on Thursday.

The suit was filed by Meta's investors and shareholders seeking up to $8B in damages, including fines of $5B by the Federal Trade Commission, after finding that the defunct political consulting firm accessed Facebook data of millions of users.

The FTC fined the company in 2019 after finding that the social media and tech giant failed to comply with a 2012 agreement with the government agency to protect users' data.

An expert witness for the plaintiffs testified on Wednesday about "gaps and weaknesses" in Facebook's privacy policies but did not say if the company violated the 2012 agreement that Facebook reached with the FTC, the report said.

The report said the parties did not disclose details of the settlement, and defense lawyers did not address the judge, Kathaleen McCormick of the Delaware Court of Chancery. McCormick adjourned the trial just as it was to enter its second day, and she congratulated the parties.

data.title: Wall Steet inches higher after the latest retail sales report and further earnings data
data.description:
Wall Street’s market averages trade higher on Thursday, as investors digested fresh data on retail sales and jobless claims.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) has pushed up by 0.2% and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) has climbed by 0.1%. At the same time, the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) has advanced by 0.5%. 

Among the 11 S&P 500 sectors, seven are up in the green, with Info Tech and Consumer Staples leading the way. At the other end of the spectrum, Health Care has been the area that has suffered the most so far. 

U.S. Treasury yields appear to be shifting slightly lower with the shorter-end U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) near even at 3.90% and the longer-end U.S. 10 Year Treasury yield (US10Y) has fallen 2 basis points to 4.43%. 

Looking out at the economic calendar and June retail sales came in at +0.6% M/M to $720.1B vs. +0.1% consensus and -0.9% prior (unrevised), while core retail sales, excluding motor vehicles and parts, came in at +0.5% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and -0.6% prior (revised from -0.3%). 

“Strong June retail sales show consumers may have been spooked by tariffs, but they haven't fully gone into hiding. The fact that June is a slower month and that goods outlays comprise a fraction of overall outlays means that we are not completely rethinking our forecast for a soft second half for consumer spending,” Wells Fargo’s Tim Quinlan said. 

Furthermore, initial jobless claims unexpectedly fell in the past week. Business inventories also held steady again in May at 0.0% M/M to $2,656.7B. Additionally, homebuilder sentiment ticked up one point in July, as was expected. 

“The VIX eased slightly overnight. Despite the back-and-forth headlines concerning the possible sacking of Fed Chair Powell and President Trump’s trade wars, market participants ultimately didn’t panic, leaving implied volatility apparently well anchored. The muted reaction to yesterday’s events suggests that traders remain focused on earnings and macro data rather than political theatre, at least for now,” David Morrison of Trade Nation said. 

Earnings remain in focus, with Travelers Corp, Citizens Financial and US Bancorp reporting. Netflix will headline after hours, offering a tech-sector litmus test amid rising subscriber growth expectations, Morrison added. 

Streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) will release its quarterly results after the closing bell.

data.title: TAO Synergies acquires $10 million of Bittensor tokens
data.description:
• TAO Synergies (NASDAQ:TAOX) +1.1% Thursday announced that as of July 17, it has acquired 29,899 TAO tokens for a total purchase price of $10 million, at an average price of about $334 per token.
  • The purchases of TAO are in accordance with the company's digital asset treasury strategy directed toward TAO, the native token for Bittensor.
  • "Spending on decentralized AI, and TAO in particular, is rising rapidly as investors seek exposure to the next phase of AI infrastructure growth," said James Altucher, digital asset treasury strategist for TAO Synergies.

data.title: Oklo on the rise after completing NRC pre-application assessment for Aurora powerhouse
data.description:
Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) +3.4% in early trading Thursday after saying it successfully completed the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission’s pre-application readiness assessment for its first commercial Aurora powerhouse project at Idaho National Laboratory.

Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) said the assessment affirmed its readiness to move forward and found no significant gaps that would prevent the NRC's acceptance of the application.

The company plans to submit the first phase of the Aurora-INL combined license application this year as part of its strategy to enable efficient and repeatable deployment of its Aurora powerhouses for commercial and federal customers.

"Our team has worked closely with the NRC to build a clear, efficient licensing pathway, and this is a good signal that we are on track," Oklo (OKLO) co-founder and CEO Jacob DeWitte said.

data.title: Fulgent Genetics gets EU approval for germline sequencing system
data.description:
• Fulgent Genetics (NASDAQ:FLGT) has received European Union’s CE certification for its germline next-generation sequencing system. 
  • This system includes FulgentExome and Fulgent Pipeline Manager, complying with the European Union’s in vitro diagnostic regulation.

data.title: Unity Software surges after Morgan Stanley says ad customers are reporting 'rapidly improving returns'
data.description:
Shares of Unity Software (NYSE:U) jumped about 12% on Thursday after Morgan Stanley noted that the company's ad customers are reporting rapidly improving returns.

The firm has an Overweight rating and a $25 price target on Unity's stock.

Analysts led by Matthew Cost said the company's customers are reporting rapidly improving returns and this leads them to believe that Unity's investments and restructuring over the past 18 months have produced a fundamentally more competitive ad product.

The analysts added that in recent weeks they have seen a marked improvement in the commentary from Unity's advertising customers and they believe that Unity Ads is delivering no less than 15% to 20% uplift on installs and purchases, i.e. the figures that management referred to at second quarter results.

"We would also reiterate that these advertisers are laser focused on ROAS [Return on Ad Spend] and we expect that U's ad customers will continue to move quickly to increase their ad budgets, as long as U's technology continues delivering stronger results," said the analysts.

The analysts highlighted three reasons why this could still be the early innings of Unity's ad inflection.

Firstly, Cost and his team said that after nearly a year of development and testing, Unity's new Vector ad model fully launched in May and after just two months we may be seeing the power of this technology shine through. Unity's scale, reach, and game engine leadership are unique assets that Vector is designed to fully leverage. As with all machine learning AI models, the analysts said they expect Vector's performance to only improve as the flywheel of more ad spend and faster training continues to spin up.

Secondly, Unity Ads just started using game engine data in July. Vector model is only as good as the data that drives its decision making. Unity's position as the leading mobile game engine in the world gives it access to data on consumer behavior that no other company can replicate and the analysts believe the company only started leveraging that data to target ads in the past two to three weeks.

The analysts noted that this seems to align closely with the improvement in customer commentary and they would expect Vector to continue improving, as it processes more and more runtime-level game data.

Lastly, Cost and his team said they believe Unity 6 adoption continues to grow strongly, as the company was already nearing 50% adoption at the second quarter results, and management noted that 80% of customers intend to upgrade. While this has the potential to drive a subscription revenue tailwind in the second half for the Create segment, it also has implications for Grow/advertising, according to the analysts.

Unity 6 brings new potential to integrate the data and sales motions across both segments. "In other words, more Unity 6 adoption likely also means more data for Unity Ads and stronger ROAS for U's advertiser customers," said the analysts.

The analysts said they remain fundamentally bullish on Unity's efforts to invest in its high-margin ad network business and leverage its unique data assets.

The analysts noted that there is still broad investor skepticism on Unity's ability to compete in advertising, "meaning there is significant potential upside to expectations and to the multiple the market will pay for U shares. To that point, we would point to our $40 bull case valuation as the potential next place for the market to focus."
